asset_name: HP Hearts and Exposure Meter Panel (220x56, 4 states)
shared_properties: The panel occupies a 220x56 px HUD footprint sized to the top-left HP and exposure readout area.
Core palette metadata remains primary #C9A84C, background #0D0B14, and accent #D4682A while heart-fill and meter colors stay inside specs.colors_hex.
Layout keeps three heart slots above a horizontal exposure bar so health loss and beam danger can be read in one glance.
differences: This state sheet includes full, damaged, critical, and exposure-full variants rather than a single static frame.
Hearts use bright fill loss and subtle crack detail, while the exposure bar pushes from amber toward exposure-white as danger rises.
A reserved numeric inset at the right edge supports optional percentage text without crowding the bar.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds HP count, exposure gauge placement, and continuous beam-danger feedback.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the panel-dark and gold-trim UI language for HUD widgets.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds readable player-danger framing around Umbra survival states.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: SpriteSheetBundle rendered by the HUD overlay camera.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load the HP-exposure panel atlas during FloorLoading and register state frames in the HUD widget registry.
runtime_notes: Anchor the widget to the top-left screen corner with whole-pixel placement.
Swap atlas frames for coarse status changes while the exposure fill amount may be masked inside the bar region.
Keep the panel above world-space camera motion and beam overlays.

context.usage: Top-left persistent HUD widget for current HP and exposure buildup state.

layout_notes: Three heart slots occupy the top 24 px band.
Right status inset width is 36 px.

acceptance_criteria: State sheet is exactly 880x56 px with four 220x56 frames and 0 px spacing.
HP loss and exposure escalation remain readable within one glance at 1920x1080 output scale.
The widget leaves enough negative space for runtime numbers without obscuring hearts or the bar fill.
